denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 02/28/2026
UnitedHealthcare Choice Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W100285084          Group #: GRP-1036
Authorization / Reference #: UM-2026-108953
Claim #: CLM-2026-189909
Date of Service (requested): 02/08/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Summit Specialty Associates

Dear Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's precertification / medical necessity review request on 02/10/2026 for Upadacitinib (Rinvoq) after methotrexate related to Rheumatoid arthritis, seropositive (M06.9). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Upadacitinib (Rinvoq) after methotrexate
Diagnosis (as submitted): Rheumatoid arthritis, seropositive (M06.9)
Denial code: CO-96 — Non-covered charge — service denied as not medically necessary under plan criteria.
Clinical policy applied: CDG-UM136 under UnitedHealthcare Coverage Determination Guideline (CDG) and applicable Medical Policy Bulletin

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CDG-UM136 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Upadacitinib (Rinvoq) after methotrexate is not considered evidence-based for Rheumatoid arthritis, seropositive (M06.9) under current medical policy. Published criteria do not support authorization.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with not evidence based. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, including plain-language descriptions, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with a UnitedHealthcare medical director. Peer-to-peer review must be requested before a formal appeal is initiated for the same authorization decision.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file, including documents, clinical criteria, and other information we considered in making this determination. Many members use this information when preparing an appeal. Submit your request to the address shown on this notice or through the member portal identified in your plan materials.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination through our internal appeals process. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W100285084) and reference UM-2026-108953. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination.

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
Clinical Operations
clinical_context: The 42-year-old member has Rheumatoid arthritis, seropositive (M06.9). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Upadacitinib (Rinvoq) after methotrexate recommended as the next appropriate step. Methotrexate 20 mg weekly ×6 months; DAS28 5.4; sulfasalazine trial failed. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that not evidence based bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if his care is delayed further.
